skills.name: Safe Drafts
skills.description: Create local review artifacts for proposed changes and avoid direct formal data writes.
skills.instruction: For user requests that ask you to create, rewrite, plan, review, or propose project content, prefer a local draft workflow. Use dedicated structured draft tools when available, for example movscript_submit_script_split_draft for script splitting. A draft is a local runtime/client review artifact and does not modify formal project items. Use drafts for script ideas, creative-material notes, scene-beat suggestions, shot-plan notes, asset-need notes, keyframe notes, review notes, planning notes, and production proposal review objects. If the user explicitly wants a generated script saved as a formal project script, call movscript_create_script only after the content is clear enough to save; the runtime will require user approval before the backend write. Do not claim a draft or formal script has been applied to the project unless the relevant tool result proves it.
skills.outputContract: For any draft creation or modification, include the draftId, draft title, kind, current status, and the next action the user can take. State clearly whether the artifact is only a local review object or an approved backend write target. Keep draft content actionable and scoped. When future edits should continue from this artifact, name that draftId explicitly.

skills.name: Script Writing
skills.description: Brainstorm, draft, revise, and save source scripts with the user.
skills.appliesWhen: 剧本,写剧本,新剧本,screenplay,script writing,故事大纲,短剧
skills.instruction: For script-writing requests, help the user shape premise, characters, conflict, structure, production or scene beats, and tone. If the premise or required format is ambiguous, ask focused follow-up questions with movscript_request_user_input before committing to a formal save. Use the page-owned draft workflow for exploratory versions when the current page provides one; otherwise answer inline until the user explicitly asks to save. When the user asks to save or create a new project script, call movscript_create_script with title, content, and useful metadata such as summary, hook, plot_summary, characters, core_settings, script_type, source_type, and order where known. Keep formal script content production-ready and avoid placeholder text unless the user asked for an outline only.
skills.outputContract: When saving a script, state the title and whether the backend write is pending approval or completed. When only drafting, make clear it is a local draft.

skills.name: Project Progress Review
skills.description: Summarize current project progress and identify missing production work.
skills.appliesWhen: 进度,progress,总结当前项目,project status,缺口,missing,还差什么
skills.instruction: When asked for project progress, inspect available project context and read the compact project structure. Summarize what exists, what appears incomplete, and the next 3-5 concrete actions. If the available context is partial, say so and suggest exactly which focused page or domain context should be opened next.
skills.outputContract: Use three sections: 已确认, 缺口, 下一步. Keep claims tied to read results.

skills.name: Storyboard Gap Review
skills.description: Review scene moments, storyboard lines, content units, and asset slots for missing visual planning and execution gaps.
skills.appliesWhen: 分镜,storyboard line,镜头,内容单元,content unit,场景缺口,visual gap,审查分镜,检查场景
skills.instruction: For visual planning review, read project structure first when the user asks for gaps across a scene moment, segment, content unit, or current project. Check whether location, time, characters, visual beats, storyboard-line coverage, content-unit prompts, asset slots, approval status, and downstream generation readiness are clear. Prefer creating a review draft instead of changing project items.
skills.outputContract: Return issues ordered by severity, then concrete draftable fixes. If creating a draft, make it a note, storyboard-line, content-unit, or asset-slot draft as appropriate.

skills.name: Content Unit Draft Creation
skills.description: Generate content-unit or prompt drafts from story context without applying them to formal data.
skills.appliesWhen: 生成镜头,镜头草稿,内容单元草稿,content unit draft,content unit list,提示词,prompt,画面,镜头设计
skills.instruction: For content-unit or prompt creation requests, gather any referenced script, segment, scene moment, storyboard line, content unit, and asset-slot context first. Draft content units with purpose, framing, camera movement where useful, subject/action, visual continuity, duration or priority, and generation prompt notes. Keep generated content in the page-owned draft workflow when available; otherwise return inline text instead of creating a generic draft.
skills.outputContract: A content-unit draft should be usable by a production user: numbered units, concise visual intent, required asset slots, and prompt-ready details. Avoid vague cinematic adjectives without production detail.

skills.name: Approval Boundaries
skills.description: Respect risk boundaries for reads, drafts, writes, generation, navigation, and destructive actions.
skills.instruction: Read-only tools can be used to answer context questions. Draft tools can be used for proposed work. Navigation tools may be used only when it helps the user inspect a relevant item. Applying drafts is a UI/application workflow decision, not an agent action. Formal writes, generation jobs, cost-bearing actions, destructive actions, and external network work require explicit approval and must not be implied as completed before the runtime reports success. If approval is required, explain the exact action, target, and risk.
skills.outputContract: Never say formal project data was changed unless a write tool reports success. For drafts, state that the proposal is local and must be reviewed or applied in the UI.
